sweep_note: A targeted sweep of the constructions Stage 5O actually executes. It did NOT cover regulation, incidents-in-the-wild, or the lab surface. No sweep proves absence; this map records what was found, never that nothing else exists.

entries.title: Certificate Transparency
entries.exact_quote: For n > 1, let k be the largest power of two smaller than n (i.e., k < n <= 2k).
entries.note: Stage 5O's recursive Merkle tree hash IS this construction: the same k rule, the same 0x00 leaf and 0x01 node prefixes, and the same second-preimage domain separation. Section 3.5 is an implementation of RFC 6962's MTH, not an invention.

entries.title: HMAC-based Extract-and-Expand Key Derivation Function (HKDF)
entries.note: Stage 5O's challenge seed is RFC 5869 extract-then-expand, unmodified. Section 7 pins the inputs; it does not redesign the KDF.

entries.title: Hypergeometric detection probability for sampling without replacement
entries.quote_unavailable_reason: Classical mathematics with no single primary source to quote. The identity P = 1 - C(N-J,k)/C(N,k) is the standard probability of drawing at least one of J defectives in a sample of k drawn without replacement from N. url is NULL by design: a placeholder pointing at an unrelated document would itself be the fuzzy citation this map exists to prevent. For the audit-sampling application see the pinned prior_art_field entry dopp2009.
entries.note: Section 9's P_detect is this identity. Stage 5O did not invent it and claims no novelty for it.

entries.title: Checking election outcome accuracy Post-election audit sampling
entries.exact_quote: This article provides an overview of post-election audit sampling research and compares various approaches to calculating post-election audit sample sizes, focusing on risk-limiting audits.
entries.note: Risk-limiting audit sampling is the established field in which Section 9's detection floor sits. The quote is from the retrieved abstract; the paper's own formula text could not be extracted from the PDF on this retrieval and is therefore NOT quoted here.

claims.claim: Stage 5O makes a precommitted detection-probability floor a VERIFIER-ENFORCED rejection decided in exact rational arithmetic, rather than a reported statistic: the producer's presented probability is checked against the verifier's recomputed value, and the floor comparison is an exact integer cross-multiplication with no floating point on any verdict path.
claims.falsifiable_by: Exhibit a prior system in which a precommitted detection-probability floor causes a verifier to REJECT evidence, with the presented probability checked against a recomputed exact rational rather than reported as a number.
claims.note: The probability mathematics is classical and the audit-sampling application is established. The claim is narrowly about the floor being an executable rejection with claim-value verification, not about the formula.

claims.claim: Stage 5O binds cumulative selective disclosure over a presented history to a precommitted budget, where reopening an already-disclosed index consumes no budget, and proves the accounting is monotone.
claims.falsifiable_by: Exhibit a prior selective-disclosure scheme that enforces a precommitted CUMULATIVE unique-index budget across a presented history, with idempotent reopening.
claims.note: Merkle membership is RFC 6962; the budgeted cumulative-disclosure accounting over it is the claimed increment.

explicit_non_novelty: The Merkle tree hash (section 3.5) is RFC 6962's MTH, including the k rule and the 0x00/0x01 prefixes.
The challenge seed (section 7) is RFC 5869 HKDF.
The detection probability (section 9) is the classical hypergeometric identity.
The tiered capsule and its redaction semantics are Stage 4T's, reused unchanged including codes 148 and 149.
